title: Beauï؟½s Reply.
content:
آ آ آ آ Sir, when I flew to seize the bird
آ آ آ آ In spite of your command,
آ آ آ آ A louder voice than yours I heard,
آ آ آ آ And harder to withstand.


آ آ آ آ You criedï؟½Forbear!ï؟½but in my breast
آ آ آ آ A mightier criedï؟½Proceed!ï؟½
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Twas nature, Sir, whose strong behest
آ آ آ آ Impellï؟½d me to the deed.


آ آ آ آ Yet, much as nature I respect,
آ آ آ آ I ventured once to break
آ آ آ آ (As you perhaps may recollect)
آ آ آ آ Her precept for your sake;


آ آ آ آ And when your linnet on a day,
آ آ آ آ Passing his prison door,
آ آ آ آ Had flutterï؟½d all his strength away,
آ آ آ آ And panting pressï؟½d the floor.


آ آ آ آ Well knowing him a sacred thing,
آ آ آ آ Not destined to my tooth,
آ آ آ آ I only kissï؟½d his ruffled wing,
آ آ آ آ And lickï؟½d the feathers smooth.


آ آ آ آ Let my obedience then excuse
آ آ آ آ My disobedience now,
آ آ آ آ Nor some reproof yourself refuse
آ آ آ آ From your aggrieved bow-wow:


آ آ آ آ If killing birds be such a crime
آ آ آ آ (Which I can hardly see),
آ آ آ آ What think you, Sir, of killing time
آ آ آ آ With verse addressï؟½d to me!
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Cowper, titled "Beauï؟½s Reply.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
